nodes.context: French-Canadian organized crime group of nine brothers operating in Montreal from 1950s to mid-1980s, involved in extortion, drug trafficking, loan sharking, and murder.
Mafia family in Montreal that rivaled the Dubois Brothers in the 1960s and 1970s.
Irish-Canadian gang specializing in truck hijackings and theft, allied with Dubois Brothers until turf war in 1973.
French biker gang controlling drug trade in Saint-Louis Square, Montreal; rival of Dubois Brothers in 1970s.
Biker gang hired by Dubois Brothers to fight Devil's Disciples; later managed drug sales in Saint-Henri square.

edges.context: The Dubois Brothers rivaled the Cotroni family in Montreal during the 1960s and 1970s.
McSween gang joined as associates in 1957, providing stolen goods in exchange for weapons and support.
Turf war broke out in 1973 over territory; Dubois Brothers emerged victorious after killing several McSween members.
Conflict over drug territory in Saint-Louis Square; Dubois hired Popeyes to eliminate Disciples, killing 15 members.
Dubois hired Popeyes as muscle to fight Devil's Disciples; Popeyes later managed drug sales in captured territory.
